headline: Both leading AI labs move toward public markets as OpenAI files confidentially for an IPO days after Anthropic.
body: OpenAI's confidential IPO filing follows Anthropic's by roughly a week, escalating the competitive dynamic between the two labs, while Sam Altman's identity verification venture Tools for Humanity is reportedly cutting staff amid revenue struggles. Apple's WWDC 2026 push dominates the product side: a new Core AI library lets developers run PyTorch models on Apple hardware, Siri features lean on vision LLMs and Gemini-derived models on Private Cloud Compute, and the company is courting smaller developers with cheaper AI access. On the technique front, a cluster of methods is breaking out together, including attribution, filter propagation, parallel execution, and noise prediction, alongside new entrant percolation and adaptive retrieval. A New York Times report that researchers were scooped on a math problem by an AI system underscores rising research automation.
